summary: No LMDB exists in the daemon code. Storage is per-project SQLite (cocoindex.db + target_sqlite.db). Q7 LMDB-corruption framing is wrong.
recommendation: Update spec.md §2 KNOWN CONTEXT to drop LMDB framing; add 'Storage model: per-project SQLite' subsection.

summary: PID 98364 was quiescent. Writes are request-driven via _dispatch->IndexRequest->update_index; with no socket binding, no requests reach PID 98364. Zero data-corruption risk from dual-daemon situation.
recommendation: No new patch required. Spec.md §2 should reframe PID 98364 as 'idle fd-leak' not 'corruption hazard'.

summary: fcntl.flock recommendation translates cleanly to msvcrt.locking on Win32. Existing daemon already maintains Win32 abstractions (AF_PIPE, OpenProcess, 8+ sys.platform branches). Lock-fd pattern eliminates Win32 PID-reuse hazard. AF_PIPE has no unlink-asymmetry, so Patch 4 (guarded unlink) is Unix-only by construction.
recommendation: Spec.md Patch 1 must include _try_acquire_pid_lock helper with sys.platform branching. Patch 4 stays inside existing 'if sys.platform != "win32":' guard at daemon.py:613.

summary: daemon.log uses plain FileHandler (daemon.py:572-575), no rotation. Live log: 23 MB / 250,485 lines / 564 BrokenPipeError events / ~40 KB per error. P0-3 fix removes 22.6 MB of error churn but legitimate INFO-level baseline is 5-10 MB/month under heavy reindex.
recommendation: Replace FileHandler with RotatingFileHandler(maxBytes=10*1024*1024, backupCount=5). Total cap 60 MB. Drop-in patch sketch in iteration-003.md §Log Rotation Recommendation.

summary: cocoindex.db (per-project, owned by cocoindex Rust binding) is opaque to Python layer. Out of spec scope but worth flagging if production sees concurrent indexers (e.g., daemon + direct CLI invocation) on the same project.
recommendation: No spec.md change. Document as 'known limitation' for follow-up packet if user reports concurrent-indexer issues.

entry: Iter 3: literal grep on lmdb|LMDB|Environment falsified Q7 framing in one tool call. Chained wc-l + grep-cE + grep-cvE composed full log forensics in single bash round. Reading actual code BEFORE reasoning from prompt's premise saves rounds.

Iter 3: almost wasted budget on CocoIndex search for hallucinated 'LMDB write path' before checking import list. Course-corrected. Carry forward: when prompt presupposes a fact, validate the fact with grep BEFORE reasoning from it.
Searching for LMDB in cocoindex_code/ — REFUTED, no LMDB import exists; storage is per-project SQLite via cocoindex.connectors.sqlite. Hypothesis 'two daemons corrupt LMDB' — REFUTED, P0-7+P0-8.
LMDB write contention — does not exist (P0-7). Dual-daemon corruption risk — does not exist (P0-8: PID 98364 quiescent, request-driven write path).

Q6 (Daemon.log growth): no rotation handler currently configured (daemon.py:572-575). 23 MB / 564 BrokenPipeError events / ~40 KB each. P0-3 fix removes ~22.6 MB. Recommended: RotatingFileHandler(maxBytes=10MB, backupCount=5). Q7 (LMDB safety): RESOLVED-NEGATIVE, no LMDB exists, storage is per-project SQLite, PID 98364 quiescent. Q8 (cross-platform): fcntl.flock translates to msvcrt.locking; lock-fd pattern eliminates Win32 PID-reuse hazard; AF_PIPE has no unlink-asymmetry so Patch 4 is Unix-only.

Iter 3: 5 findings (3 P0, 1 P1, 1 P2). Q6+Q7+Q8 answered. P0-7 + P0-8 reframe Q7 as RESOLVED-NEGATIVE (no LMDB, PID 98364 quiescent). P0-9 validates cross-platform translation with concrete msvcrt.locking sketch. P1-3 supplies RotatingFileHandler drop-in patch. P2-3 flags cocoindex.db Rust-binding opacity as out-of-scope. Convergence delta = -0.0 (held at 0.80 → 0.85 with reframing). Q9 only remaining.
Iter 3: 5 findings (3 P0, 1 P1, 1 P2), novelty 0.85, delta +0.05 vs iter 2. Stop reason: Q9 still open, continue.

Iter 4 priorities: Q9 (socket backlog) - 1-2 tool calls; consolidate full spec.md correction list (9 P0+P1, 3 P2 findings) for iter 5 synthesis; validate cross-platform helper against mcp_server/tests/ Win32 fixtures. If iter 4 novelty <=0.10, declare convergence and bring iter 5 synthesis forward.
